macro_segments.title: Wrapping up the prohibition against owning chametz during sacrificial slaughter and the scope of liability

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the prohibition of lo tishchat al chametz and its scriptural basis
The Baraita discussion of who transgresses: shochet, zorek, malik, and mazeh in relation to chametz ownership
Resolving contradictions between two Baraitot regarding melika and haktarah
Rabbi Shimon's position distinguishing erev Pesach from Pesach itself regarding types of sacrifices
The scriptural basis for Rabbi Yehuda's inclusion of the tamid and Rabbi Shimon's limitation to Pesach alone
Rabbi Shimon's ruling on shechting a second Pesach during moed and the status of the korban when slaughtered shelo lishmo

macro_segments.title: The procedure and logistics of bringing the Korban Pesach with three groups and the assembly-line process of blood collection

micro_segments.title: The division of Israel into three groups and the three terms in the verse indicating this division
Locking the Azarah when full and the shofar blasts marking each group's rotation
The arrangement of kohanim in rows holding silver and gold bowls without bases to prevent blood from congealing
Yisrael shochting and kohanim receiving and passing the blood in a bucket-brigade fashion to the mizbeach
The location of the throwing of blood against the wall near the base and the spatial constraints of the mizbeach
Reading of Hallel during and between the groups' offerings and the limitation of the third group's participation

macro_segments.title: The continuation of the Pesach procedure on Shabbat, the flaying and preparation of the offering, and the waiting of groups

micro_segments.title: The permission to flay on Shabbat and the concerns about washing the Azarah on Shabbat
The use of iron hooks and smooth poles for hanging and flaying the Pesach in the Beit HaMitbachayim
Rabbi Eliezer's ruling using human arms instead of poles when the 14th falls on Shabbat
The removal of innards and fats, their burning on the altar, and the departure of the first group
The location of the groups during Shabbat: first group on Har HaBayit, second in the Cheil, third in the Azarah

macro_segments.title: Talmudic analysis of the minimum number of people required for a Pesach group and the debate between Abaye and Rava on locking the Azarah

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yitzchak's requirement of three groups of thirty people each and the solution of overlapping groups
Abaye's position that the doors locked themselves miraculously versus Rava's position that they were actively locked
The application of the locking debate to the story of Akavya ben Mahalalel and his exclusion from the community

macro_segments.title: The miraculous crowding in the Azarah, the story of King Agrippas counting Pesachim by kidneys, and the population estimate

micro_segments.title: The claim that no one was ever crushed except once in the time of Hillel
King Agrippas' method of counting Pesachim through kidney collection and the resulting census of 600,000 pairs
Estimation of population capacity based on the Azarah's dimensions and the theological significance of the crowding
Talmudic clarification that the kidneys were taken as a marking device and then returned with the sacrifice

macro_segments.title: Explanation of practical details regarding the bowls, the aesthetics of the arrangement, and the act of transport in the blood-passing procedure

micro_segments.title: Why all bowls in the Temple had no bases to prevent blood from congealing, except those used for frankincense
The non-mixing of gold and silver bowls as an aesthetic choice for beauty and honor
The question of whether passing blood hand-to-hand constitutes the required act of transport (holacha) to the mizbeach
The resolution that the procedure demonstrates the honor and beauty of involving many kohanim in the sacred service

topical_tags.term: shelo lishmo (not for its intended purpose)
